name: Hennessy Capital Investment Corp. VII
business: HVII is a special purpose acquisition company formed to acquire one or more businesses, focusing on the industrial technology and energy transition sectors. It has entered into a business combination agreement with ONE Nuclear, a developer of large-scale energy solutions using natural gas and advanced nuclear SMR technologies. HVII has no operational revenue and will not engage in operations until it completes its initial business combination.
sellsTo: Public investors in its initial public offering

exposures.sentence: HVII intends to focus on industries that complement its management team's background, and to capitalize on the ability of its management team to identify and acquire a business, focusing on the industrial technology and energy transition sectors.

HVII intends to focus on industries that complement its management team's background, and to capitalize on the ability of its management team to identify and acquire a business, focusing on the industrial technology and energy transition sectors.
ONE Nuclear is an independent developer of large-scale energy solutions powered by natural gas and advanced nuclear small modular reactor (SMR) technologies.

catalysts.event: Signing of Business Combination Agreement with ONE Nuclear
catalysts.sentence: On October 22, 2025, HVII, Merger Sub and ONE Nuclear entered into a business combination agreement (as may be amended or supplemented from time to time, the Business Combination Agreement), which contemplates an all-stock business combination transaction and aggregate consideration of $1.0 billion payable to the ONE Nuclear Members.

HVII, as registrant, and ONE Nuclear, as co-registrant, filed a registration statement on Form S-4 (File No. 333-292440) (as may be amended and supplemented from time to time, the S-4 Registration Statement), with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the SEC) on December 23, 2025.

catalysts.event: Deadline to complete initial business combination
catalysts.sentence: HVII has up to 24 months from the closing of its initial public offering to consummate an initial business combination.
